mechanism_narrative: A lower federal funds rate directly reduces the discount rate in equity valuation models, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The correlation between FEDFUNDS and 20-day forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05) over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 9 percent signals severe labor market slack that depresses consumer spending and corporate profit expectations, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 20-day returns in more than 50 percent of months when UNRATE exceeds 9 percent during any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad-based contraction in aggregate demand that is not captured by either series in isolation, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month change negative and HOUST 3-month change negative
failure_condition: The bivariate interaction of RSAFS and HOUST does not yield a negative mean 60-day return (t-test p > 0.05) when both series decline jointly over any 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth predicts positive S&P 500 returns when monetary policy is accommodative because easy credit amplifies the pass-through from output gains to earnings; when the federal funds rate is below 1 percent, rising INDPRO drives positive 20-day returns.
failure_condition: During periods with FEDFUNDS below 1 percent, the correlation between INDPRO changes and 20-day S&P 500 returns is not positive and statistically significant (p > 0.10) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales directly foreshadows weaker near-term corporate revenues and triggers immediate risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day return frequency is less than 55 percent following month-over-month RSAFS declines over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Falling nonfarm payrolls combined with a rising unemployment rate confirm a durable labor market deterioration that erodes household income and aggregate demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal from PAYEMS and UNRATE fails to produce a statistically negative mean 60-day return (t-test p > 0.05) over any 5-year walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held below 25 basis points reflects maximal monetary accommodation that suppresses short-term funding costs and induces positive drift in the S&P 500 over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day return frequency is less than or equal to 50 percent during triggered periods over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in quarterly real GDP signals robust aggregate demand that translates into upward earnings revisions, lifting the S&P 500 over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between GDPC1 growth and 20-day forward returns is not positive and significant (p > 0.10) over any 5-year rolling window.
